edges.candidate: Search query is empty/whitespace, or the product list itself is empty — does it return all products, no products, or an error?
Searching 'cafe' against a product named 'café' (and 'CAFÉ') — is matching case-insensitive and accent/diacritic- and Unicode-normalization-insensitive, or byte-exact?
When multiple products match the query equally well (e.g. two products both named exactly 'Widget'), is the result order specified and stable across repeated searches?
A product priced exactly at the min and exactly at the max of the range — are the bounds inclusive or exclusive, and what about a price one cent below min / above max?
Prices stored as floating point (e.g. 19.99) filtered against a range bound of 20.00 — is rounding/representation defined so 19.999... does not slip past the boundary?
Category with no products, an inverted range (min > max), or no filters selected at all — does it return empty, all results, or an error?
Among products that pass both category and price filters, is the order of the filtered result set specified and stable?
